Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

firewall: replace ipset with tinyproxy domain-based filtering

Switch from IP-based allow lists (ipset + iptables) to a tinyproxy
forward proxy with domain-based filtering. Only tinyproxy can make
direct outbound 80/443 connections; all other processes must go
through the proxy.

- init-firewall.sh: configure tinyproxy with allowed domains, write
  proxy env vars to /tmp/proxy.env, support project-specific domains
  via .devcontainer/allowed-domains.txt
- dc-build: install tinyproxy in the DC wrapper image
- dc-lib: source /tmp/proxy.env after firewall init

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: complete Rogue 3.6 JS port — 22/22 sessions at 100% screen parity

Port C Rogue 3.6 to JavaScript (rogue/js/) with full test harness
(rogue/test/) achieving identical screen output to the C reference on
all 22 reference sessions.

Key bugs fixed during this session:
- things.js: NUMTHINGS/MAXPOTIONS/etc were undefined (accessed via g.
  instead of imported from const.js); caused all floor items to be
  potions (pick_one loop j < undefined never ran)
- node_runner.mjs: _setPackDeps used detach_pack_fn instead of
  detach_lvl_fn; caused pack corruption when picking up floor items
- weapons.js / curses.js / io.js: module-level state (_grpnum, cursor
  state, msgbuf) not reset between sessions in --all mode; caused
  cross-session item group collisions and cursor artifacts
- move.js / node_runner.mjs / main.js: g.player.t_pos was replaced
  with a new object on each move; monsters stored t_dest = old object
  and chased stale position. Fixed by mutating t_pos in-place so all
  t_dest references track current player position (mirrors C pointer
  semantics for &hero)
- chase.js: rer was not set to null when monster stood on a DOOR tile
  (C: rer = NULL); caused wrong room-exit-seeking logic for monsters
  on doors
- chase.js: do_chase attack check compared ch_ret to player position
  instead of thisPos (intermediate destination); mirrors C ce(this,hero)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(poe2): add POE v2 skill library — base protocol + 8 specialist skills

Closes bp6-pdr.6 and bp6-pdr.7.

poe-base.md: universal protocol inherited by all v2 skills. Covers non-interactive
rules, poe: event wire format, artifact-task sync rule, poe:review and poe:decision
usage semantics, artifact type catalogue, and required event sequence.

senior-engineer.md: reactive reviewer skill. Invoked via poe:review from peer agents
mid-task — not assigned a lifecycle stage. Entry condition is "received a poe:review
request". Produces a single decisive review artifact with APPROVED/APPROVED WITH
CONDITIONS/BLOCKED verdict and exits.

Guardrails stage specialists:
- architecture-analyst: produces architecture-constraints.md
- interface-analyst: produces interface-control.md
- data-model-analyst: produces data-model.md (cross-references interface-control)
- must-not-analyst: produces must-nots.md with rationale and verification criteria

Plan quality specialists:
- plan-reviewer: inner loop Check — reviews phase plan against guardrails artifact
  corpus before execution; uses poe:review to pull in senior-engineer for technical
  questions; emits phase-N-plan-review.md with structured verdict

Retrospective specialists:
- validity-analyst: outer loop Check — compares C' against C! using gap registry
- rca-analyst: outer loop Act — attributes root causes via f(C,T,S,K,H) model,
  emits poe:knowledge corrections, produces phase-N-rca.md

All skills inherit poe-base.md and land in poe2/src-tauri/skills/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
opus-2026-03-07-S33: k-colored independence polynomial I_k(Omega, x)

I_k(Omega, x) = A(n,k) + sum_I c_k * x^{parts} * I(T)
Satisfies: I_k(Omega, 2) = a_k(T), I_0 = I_{n-1} = I(Omega, x).
Trivariate GF: G(t,x) = A_n(t) + sum_I x^parts I(T) A_{f+1}(t) (t-1)^{d-f}
Point (0, 2) = H(T), line x=0 = Eulerian polynomial.

fix: 🐛 verify cert issuance before running install-cert

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(install): enhance install script with best practices (Refs #182)

Features added:
- SHA256 checksum verification
- Backup existing installation before upgrade
- Dry-run mode (-n)
- Verbose mode (-V) and quiet mode (-q)
- Force reinstall (-f)
- Skip verification (--skip-verify)
- Retry mechanism for downloads (3 attempts)
- Better error handling with trap/cleanup
- Auto-generate secure API key
- Detect running process before uninstall
- NO_COLOR environment variable support

if needed, then text-only as last resort.

Fix 💬 badge visibility and Torrentio 403

- Subs badge: move 💬 outside the size block so it renders even when
  no file size is detected; previously it was appended to the size
  string and silently dropped when gb == 0
- Torrentio 403: add torrentio back to SESSION_SENSITIVE_HOSTS so the
  client IP is forwarded — Vercel's shared egress IP was being blocked
  by Torrentio's WAF/rate-limiter; forwarding the real user IP resolves it

commit.message:
Add web frontend, backtest system, and recommendation engine

- Web frontend: vanilla HTML/CSS/JS SPA served from FastAPI
  - Dashboard with BUY/HOLD/SELL grouped recommendation cards
  - Stock detail with chart (TradingView), technical, financial, news tabs
  - Backtest page with hold-period analysis table and insights
  - Dark theme, responsive design, Korean UI
- Backtest engine: per-ticker sliding window simulation with confidence tiers
- Recommendation scoring: technical + financial + volume + momentum + news
- Sentiment analysis via news headlines
- Hold-period cache (8h) keyed by hold_days, cache clear API

commit.message:
fix: Strip "ISO " prefix from rexif value_more_readable for ISO extraction (#174)

rexif formats ISOSpeedRatings as "ISO 500" not "500", so parse::<u32>()
was silently failing. Refactor extract_camera_info, extract_capture_date,
and extract_location_info to free functions for direct testability.

Use mix-blend-mode screen on poster images instead of chromakey

Chromakey was removing dark parts of the product. Instead, extract frames
with their original black background and use CSS mix-blend-mode: screen
to make black invisible — preserves all product detail perfectly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

camera: raise stream fps to 15 (modal open = robot stopped)

When the video modal is open the user cannot drive the robot, so stream
CPU saturation has no impact on navigation. 15 fps gives smoother video.
Detection-only mode stays at 2 fps for minimal patrol CPU impact.

generate predictions without any market data. Now throws

commit.message:
Migrate review and dashboard to SQLite; add template and import APIs

Backend:
- Added Template struct with CreateTemplate, GetTemplates, GetTemplate,
  DeleteTemplate in database.go
- Added GET/POST /api/databases/:id/templates and DELETE .../templates/:id
- Added POST /api/databases/:id/transactions/import for bulk CSV import

Frontend:
- dashboard.js: renderStats() is now async, fetches transactions/categories/
  templates in parallel via API; template management uses TemplateAPI;
  CSV import uses TransactionAPI.importMany()
- review.js: loadUnreviewedTransactions() uses TransactionAPI.getAll()
  filtered by !reviewed; saveTransaction() uses TransactionAPI.update()
  with splits JSON-stringified; removed all DatabaseManager usage

All application data now lives in SQLite. localStorage only retains the
active database ID as a session pointer.

- Updated CLAUDE.md to reflect completed backend migration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
kind-pasteur-2026-03-07-S27: computation scripts for W(r) analysis

- free_position_universality.py/v2: F_f != avg W (both models fail)
- positivity_constraints.py: a_k=0 only at n=3; n>=4 always positive
- t3_t5_constraint_n7.py: n=5 identity doesn't generalize; W(0) frac=3/4
- W_complex_evals.py: W(i/2) real at odd n; =0 for Paley T_3, T_7
- skeleton_n7_eigenvalues.py: n=7 skeleton eigenvalue computation
- deformed_eulerian_properties.py: inflated coefficient analysis
- free_position_universality_proof.py: position-independence test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pin postgres volume to butlers_postgres_data

After machine restart, Docker compose project rename (butlers→rig)
caused a new empty rig_postgres_data volume to be created instead of
reusing the existing butlers_postgres_data volume where all butler
schemas live. Declare postgres_data as an external volume pointing to
butlers_postgres_data so future restarts always mount the correct data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
